title: What businesses in Boston are worth waiting in line for?
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brandy Melville on Newbury Street attracts frequent lines due to its unique "one size fits all" clothing policy.
Blank Street Coffee on Boylston Street is known for its long queues, necessitating line security at the location.
Brick Street Bagels in South Boston draws considerable crowds, despite mixed reviews on social media.

title: Mass. woman pleads guilty to performing medical spa injections using counterfeit products
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli, 40, of Stoughton, pleaded guilty to using counterfeit products for cosmetic injections at her Massachusetts spas.
Fadanelli imported fake Botox and fillers from China and Brazil, earning over $1 million despite lacking proper licensing.
She could face up to 20 years in prison and $250,000 fines; clients are urged to report counterfeit services to the FDA.

title: Man fatally shot by Boston police near Northeastern University campus identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jacob Graves, 29, was fatally shot by Boston police after wielding a 'sword-like weapon' during a mental health crisis.
The incident occurred near Northeastern University and resulted in non-life-threatening injuries to multiple officers and emergency responders.
Recent violent incidents near Northeastern have led to protests from students and faculty over communication from the university.

title: Celtics’ Brad Stevens addresses ‘tiresome’ college basketball rumors
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Brad Stevens is not planning to leave the Celtics despite interest from college basketball programs.
Stevens was rumored to be on UNC's shortlist for a coaching position before Mike Malone was hired.
Stevens remains committed to his role with the Celtics, emphasizing contentment and stability in his current position.

title: Man gets life sentence in 2024 killing of Boston father of 2
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Widmaer Lapaix, 29, sentenced to life with parole possibility after 15 years for killing Maceo Martin in 2024.
Martin, 41, was shot outside a Cambridge apartment during a dispute involving his ex-wife and Lapaix.
Lapaix convicted of second-degree murder following a nine-day trial; apologized to Martin's family at sentencing.

title: Lindsay Clancy will admit to killing her children if trial is split into 2 parts, defense says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's defense seeks a two-phase trial, admitting to killings if granted, focusing on her mental state.
Judge William Sullivan denied the request, stating guilt and responsibility should be addressed simultaneously in a single trial.
Clancy is accused of strangling her three children in 2023; her trial is scheduled to start on July 20.

title: Patriots trade Marte Mapu to Houston Texans
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots trade linebacker Marte Mapu to Houston Texans for a 2027 sixth-round pick, sending a seventh-rounder with him.
Mapu, drafted 76th overall in 2023, played 17 games last season, focusing mainly on special teams and defense.
New England gains $1.5 million cap space and freed a roster spot, seeking linebacker upgrades for the 2026 season.

title: Suspicious person prompts lockdown, canceled classes at R.I. college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Classes at Community College of Rhode Island's Warwick campus were canceled due to a suspicious man with a suspected weapon.
A lockdown was implemented after a man with a backpack, appearing to carry a gun, entered nearby woods.
Police found an airsoft gun and pellet rifle in a backpack but have not yet located the person of interest.

title: Red Sox get more bad news after Triston Casas suffers setback in his recovery
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Triston Casas' recovery from a torn patellar tendon has been derailed by a recent rib muscle strain.
Casas was expected to resume hitting but experienced a setback, requiring further imaging this week.
Since his breakout 2023 season, Casas has played only 92 games due to ongoing injuries.

title: What to know about the ‘massive’ military bunker beneath Trump’s ballroom
author: New York Times Service
bullets: President Trump's $400 million ballroom project includes a massive underground military bunker complex.
The bunker will feature bomb shelters, medical facilities, secure communications, and bioweapons defenses.
Judge Richard J. Leon halted the ballroom project, citing the need for congressional approval, despite ongoing security-related construction.
